title: Sparse Signal Recovery (analysis / synthesis sparsity)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Donoho-Elad 2003 uniqueness; Candes-Romberg-Tao 2006 robust recovery; Daubechies-Defrise-DeMol 2004 iterative thresholding; Elad 2010 Sparse and Redundant Representations book.

E.description: A signal x admits sparse representation alpha in some dictionary D such that x = D*alpha (synthesis model) or Omega*x is sparse (analysis model). Observations y = A*x + n are measured with a linear operator A. The task is to recover x via sparsity-constrained optimization.
E.forward_model: y = A * D * alpha + n;  ||alpha||_0 <= k or x such that ||Omega*x||_0 <= k
E.world_state_x: Signal x in R^N with sparse coefficients in dictionary D in R^{N x K}
physical_parameters_theta: Measurement operator A (identity = denoising; random = CS)

W.regime: Unique sparsity under mutual-coherence or RIP bounds; relaxed L0->L1 equivalence under Donoho-Elad theorem. Mismatch: dictionary drift, non-exact sparsity, off-grid sparsity (basis mismatch).

C.solver_class: OMP, MP, CoSaMP, BPDN/LASSO, FISTA, ISTA, HTP, IRLS, learned (LISTA, ALISTA)
C.error_bound: ||x - x_hat||_2 <= C1 sigma sqrt(k log N / M) + C2 ||x - x_k||_1 / sqrt(k)
C.complexity: OMP: O(k*M*N); FISTA: O(N*M) per iter with O(1/k^2) convergence
